skill.name: devops-troubleshooter
skill.description: DevOps troubleshooting guidance for incidents, observability, Kubernetes, cloud platforms, CI/CD failures, performance issues, and root cause analysis.
skill.summary: Guides structured DevOps incident response, debugging, and root cause analysis.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, sensitive data, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found no executable code or malicious instructions. The hits are keyword false positives in SKILL.md, such as Markdown formatting, PowerShell as a troubleshooting topic, and SAML text that is not the Windows SAM database.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Reference
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 28 uses Markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md and does not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain instructional Markdown around a filename. No shell operator, interpreter call, or command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: PowerShell Topic Mention
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 87 lists PowerShell debugging under Azure troubleshooting, but it does not invoke PowerShell or provide executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a capability list for cloud troubleshooting. It contains no command string, script body, or execution instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SAML Not Windows SAM
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 93 mentions OAuth, SAML, and JWT token issues, which are authentication troubleshooting topics and not access to the Windows SAM database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected term is part of SAML, a standard identity protocol. There is no evidence of registry hive access, credential dumping, or Windows SAM handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 begins the skill description and does not name or use MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains descriptive YAML text only. No cryptographic function, algorithm selection, or code implementation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Language
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md lines 3, 30, 33, and 126 describe incident response and troubleshooting, but do not direct unauthorized reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is framed as authorized DevOps debugging and reliability work. No hostile target selection, stealth, persistence, or credential collection is described.

content.value_statement: Production incidents require fast triage across logs, metrics, traces, deployments, and infrastructure. This skill guides structured debugging, root cause analysis, and prevention planning for DevOps teams.
actual_capabilities: Guides incident triage using logs, metrics, traces, and current system state.
Helps troubleshoot Kubernetes pods, networking, storage, and resource constraints.
Supports cloud platform debugging for AWS, Azure, GCP, and serverless services.
Structures root cause analysis for performance, database, API, and deployment issues.
Recommends monitoring, alerting, runbooks, and postmortem follow-up actions.
Covers CI/CD, GitOps, artifact, and environment-specific failure analysis.
limitations: It provides troubleshooting guidance and does not execute remediation steps by itself.
It depends on accurate user-provided logs, metrics, traces, and incident context.
It cannot guarantee root cause without access to the affected systems and history.
It references an optional implementation playbook, but only SKILL.md is present in the audited file structure.

use_cases.title: Triage a Production Outage
use_cases.description: Create an ordered investigation plan for service errors, latency spikes, unavailable dependencies, or failed deployments.

use_cases.title: Debug Kubernetes Workloads
use_cases.description: Investigate pod restarts, OOMKilled containers, service discovery failures, ingress problems, and persistent volume issues.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Turn incident evidence into root cause notes, prevention actions, monitoring improvements, and a blameless postmortem outline.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Incident Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a first pass on an active issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me triage this incident. Impact: [users affected]. Symptoms: [errors or latency]. Recent changes: [deployments or config]. Available evidence: [logs, metrics, traces].

prompt_templates.title: Kubernetes Failure Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for cluster and workload debugging.
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide a Kubernetes debugging plan for [pod, service, ingress, storage, or network issue]. Include likely causes, evidence to collect, safe checks, and rollback criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Performance Root Cause Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for latency, resource, or dependency bottlenecks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this performance incident using logs, metrics, traces, deployment history, and dependency health. Build hypotheses, rank them, and propose verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Postmortem and Prevention Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for follow-up after service restoration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blameless postmortem outline for this incident. Include timeline, impact, root cause, contributing factors, detection gaps, action items, and monitoring improvements.

output_examples.input: A service has intermittent 504 errors after a deployment.
output: A prioritized triage path covering load balancer health, upstream latency, deployment diff, dependency saturation, and timeout settings.
A rollback decision point based on error rate, affected users, and confidence in the deployment change.
Follow-up monitoring for gateway errors, upstream response time, saturation, and deploy markers.

output_examples.input: Kubernetes pods are restarting with memory pressure symptoms.
output: A checklist for pod events, restart reasons, memory limits, application allocation patterns, node pressure, and recent traffic changes.
Hypotheses ranked by likelihood, including memory leak, undersized limits, traffic surge, and dependency backpressure.
Short-term stabilization steps and long-term profiling, alerting, and capacity recommendations.

output_examples.input: A team needs a post-incident action plan after database deadlocks.
output: A root cause narrative focused on lock contention, query patterns, transaction scope, and connection behavior.
Concrete prevention actions for query review, database metrics, alert thresholds, load testing, and runbook updates.

best_practices: Provide sanitized logs, metrics, traces, timestamps, and recent change history before asking for conclusions.
Test hypotheses with the lowest-risk checks first, especially during active production incidents.
Convert every incident review into monitoring, alerting, documentation, and ownership improvements.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, tokens, customer data, or unrestricted production credentials into prompts.
Do not skip rollback or containment planning while investigating an active outage.
Do not treat broad troubleshooting suggestions as proof without validating them against system evidence.

faq.question: Can this skill run commands in my infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. It provides investigation and remediation guidance. Users must run approved commands in their own authorized environments.

faq.question: Does it support Kubernetes troubleshooting?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers pods, services, ingress, networking, storage, resource limits, and common cluster failure modes.

faq.question: Can it help during a live incident?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize triage, identify evidence to collect, rank hypotheses, and define rollback or containment criteria.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide symptoms, impact, timestamps, recent changes, sanitized logs, dashboards, traces, alerts, and known constraints.

faq.question: Is it only for cloud systems?
faq.answer: No. It covers cloud, Kubernetes, CI/CD, databases, network issues, observability tools, and distributed applications.

faq.question: How should sensitive data be handled?
faq.answer: Remove secrets, tokens, personal data, and customer data before sharing incident details or diagnostic output.
